title: Gratuitous .expect() in private_key_to_mnemonic
description:
In `did.rs:37-38`:

```rust
let mnemonic = bip39::Mnemonic::from_entropy(private_key)
    .expect("32 bytes is valid BIP-39 entropy");
```

This `.expect()` is technically safe — the BIP-39 spec guarantees 32 bytes is valid entropy, and the function signature enforces `&[u8; 32]`. The panic can never trigger in practice.

However, per the project's AGENTS.md rule "No `.unwrap()` in any code path reachable from a network request" — this function is only called from CLI paths (not network request paths), so it's acceptable. Documenting this as informational only; no action needed unless the function is ever called from a request handler.
close_reason: Acceptable: .expect() on 32-byte BIP-39 entropy which is always valid
